## Immediate Priority (Next Phase)

### 🏗️ 1. ForgeScript Language Design & Initial Runtime (KER-004)
**Status**: Ready to Start  
**Assigned to**: Developer  
**Due**: Sprint 2 completion  
**Priority**: Critical  
**Story Points**: 13

**Description:** Design and implement the initial ForgeScript language features as a TypeScript fluent API for building psychology experiments.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] Design core ForgeScript API structure (Timeline, Events, Stimuli, Responses)
- [ ] Implement Timeline class with hierarchical support
- [ ] Build Event system for experiment flow control
- [ ] Create Stimulus primitives (Text, Image, Audio, Video)
- [ ] Implement Response collectors (Keyboard, Mouse, Touch)
- [ ] Build experiment configuration and metadata system
- [ ] Create experiment compilation and validation
- [ ] Design experiment serialization format

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Simple experiment can be authored using TypeScript fluent API
- [ ] Timeline supports nested blocks and parallel execution
- [ ] Stimulus timing is frame-accurate with PrecisionClock integration
- [ ] Response collection works with DeviceManager integration
- [ ] Experiments can be serialized to/from JSON format
- [ ] Runtime validates experiment structure before execution
- [ ] Error messages provide clear debugging information
- [ ] TypeScript provides full intellisense and type checking

**Package Structure:**
```
packages/forgescript/
├── src/
│   ├── core/
│   │   ├── Timeline.ts
│   │   ├── Event.ts
│   │   ├── Experiment.ts
│   │   └── Runtime.ts
│   ├── stimuli/
│   │   ├── Text.ts
│   │   ├── Image.ts
│   │   ├── Audio.ts
│   │   └── Video.ts
│   ├── responses/
│   │   ├── KeyboardResponse.ts
│   │   ├── MouseResponse.ts
│   │   └── TouchResponse.ts
│   ├── serialization/
│   │   ├── Serializer.ts
│   │   └── Validator.ts
│   └── index.ts
├── tests/
├── examples/
└── package.json
```

**Example API:**
```typescript
const experiment = new Experiment()
  .timeline([
    new Timeline('welcome')
      .add(new TextStimulus('Welcome to the study!')
        .duration(2000))
      .add(new KeyboardResponse()
        .keys(['space'])
        .message('Press space to continue')),
    
    new Timeline('trial-loop')
      .repeat(10)
      .randomize()
      .add(new TextStimulus()
        .text(trial => trial.stimulus)
        .duration(1000))
      .add(new KeyboardResponse()
        .keys(['f', 'j'])
        .timeout(5000))
  ]);

await experiment.run();
```

**Dependencies**: @psyforge/timing, @psyforge/scheduling, @psyforge/devices  
**Risk Level**: High (core API design affects entire platform)  
**Notes**: Foundation language for all experiments - must be intuitive yet powerful

### 🏗️ 1. ForgeScript Language Design & Initial Runtime (KER-004)
**Status**: Ready to Start  
**Assigned to**: Developer  
**Due**: Sprint 2 completion  
**Priority**: Critical  
**Story Points**: 13

**Description:** Design and implement the initial ForgeScript language features as a TypeScript fluent API for building psychology experiments.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] Design core ForgeScript API structure (Timeline, Events, Stimuli, Responses)
- [ ] Implement Timeline class with hierarchical support
- [ ] Build Event system for experiment flow control
- [ ] Create Stimulus primitives (Text, Image, Audio, Video)
- [ ] Implement Response collectors (Keyboard, Mouse, Touch)
- [ ] Build experiment configuration and metadata system
- [ ] Create experiment compilation and validation
- [ ] Design experiment serialization format

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Simple experiment can be authored using TypeScript fluent API
- [ ] Timeline supports nested blocks and parallel execution
- [ ] Stimulus timing is frame-accurate with PrecisionClock integration
- [ ] Response collection works with DeviceManager integration
- [ ] Experiments can be serialized to/from JSON format
- [ ] Runtime validates experiment structure before execution
- [ ] Error messages provide clear debugging information
- [ ] TypeScript provides full intellisense and type checking

**Package Structure:**
```
packages/forgescript/
├── src/
│   ├── core/
│   │   ├── Timeline.ts
│   │   ├── Event.ts
│   │   ├── Experiment.ts
│   │   └── Runtime.ts
│   ├── stimuli/
│   │   ├── Text.ts
│   │   ├── Image.ts
│   │   ├── Audio.ts
│   │   └── Video.ts
│   ├── responses/
│   │   ├── KeyboardResponse.ts
│   │   ├── MouseResponse.ts
│   │   └── TouchResponse.ts
│   ├── serialization/
│   │   ├── Serializer.ts
│   │   └── Validator.ts
│   └── index.ts
├── tests/
├── examples/
└── package.json
```

**Example API:**
```typescript
const experiment = new Experiment()
  .timeline([
    new Timeline('welcome')
      .add(new TextStimulus('Welcome to the study!')
        .duration(2000))
      .add(new KeyboardResponse()
        .keys(['space'])
        .message('Press space to continue')),
    
    new Timeline('trial-loop')
      .repeat(10)
      .randomize()
      .add(new TextStimulus()
        .text(trial => trial.stimulus)
        .duration(1000))
      .add(new KeyboardResponse()
        .keys(['f', 'j'])
        .timeout(5000))
  ]);

await experiment.run();
```

**Dependencies**: @psyforge/timing, @psyforge/scheduling, @psyforge/devices  
**Risk Level**: High (core API design affects entire platform)  
**Notes**: Foundation language for all experiments - must be intuitive yet powerful

### 🏗️ 1. ForgeScript Language Design & Initial Runtime (KER-004)
**Status**: Ready to Start  
**Assigned to**: Developer  
**Due**: Sprint 2 completion  
**Priority**: Critical  
**Story Points**: 13

**Description:** Design and implement the initial ForgeScript language features as a TypeScript fluent API for building psychology experiments.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] Design core ForgeScript API structure (Timeline, Events, Stimuli, Responses)
- [ ] Implement Timeline class with hierarchical support
- [ ] Build Event system for experiment flow control
- [ ] Create Stimulus primitives (Text, Image, Audio, Video)
- [ ] Implement Response collectors (Keyboard, Mouse, Touch)
- [ ] Build experiment configuration and metadata system
- [ ] Create experiment compilation and validation
- [ ] Design experiment serialization format

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Simple experiment can be authored using TypeScript fluent API
- [ ] Timeline supports nested blocks and parallel execution
- [ ] Stimulus timing is frame-accurate with PrecisionClock integration
- [ ] Response collection works with DeviceManager integration
- [ ] Experiments can be serialized to/from JSON format
- [ ] Runtime validates experiment structure before execution
- [ ] Error messages provide clear debugging information
- [ ] TypeScript provides full intellisense and type checking

**Package Structure:**
```
packages/forgescript/
├── src/
│   ├── core/
│   │   ├── Timeline.ts
│   │   ├── Event.ts
│   │   ├── Experiment.ts
│   │   └── Runtime.ts
│   ├── stimuli/
│   │   ├── Text.ts
│   │   ├── Image.ts
│   │   ├── Audio.ts
│   │   └── Video.ts
│   ├── responses/
│   │   ├── KeyboardResponse.ts
│   │   ├── MouseResponse.ts
│   │   └── TouchResponse.ts
│   ├── serialization/
│   │   ├── Serializer.ts
│   │   └── Validator.ts
│   └── index.ts
├── tests/
├── examples/
└── package.json
```

**Example API:**
```typescript
const experiment = new Experiment()
  .timeline([
    new Timeline('welcome')
      .add(new TextStimulus('Welcome to the study!')
        .duration(2000))
      .add(new KeyboardResponse()
        .keys(['space'])
        .message('Press space to continue')),
    
    new Timeline('trial-loop')
      .repeat(10)
      .randomize()
      .add(new TextStimulus()
        .text(trial => trial.stimulus)
        .duration(1000))
      .add(new KeyboardResponse()
        .keys(['f', 'j'])
        .timeout(5000))
  ]);

await experiment.run();
```

**Dependencies**: @psyforge/timing, @psyforge/scheduling, @psyforge/devices  
**Risk Level**: High (core API design affects entire platform)  
**Notes**: Foundation language for all experiments - must be intuitive yet powerful

# Next Tasks Queue - Psycho-Forge Development

**Project:** Psycho-Forge - The Scientific Operating System for Behavioral Research  
**Current Phase:** Pre-Development Planning  
**Last Updated:** July 2025

---

## Immediate Priority (Next Phase)

### 🏗️ 1. Nx Monorepo Setup & CI/CD Pipeline (KER-005)
**Status**: Ready to Start  
**Assigned to**: Developer  
**Due**: Before Sprint 1 begins  
**Priority**: Critical  
**Story Points**: 8

**Description:** Set up the complete development environment with Nx monorepo tooling and comprehensive CI/CD pipeline to support multi-package development.

**Technical Requirements:**
- [ ] Initialize Nx workspace with TypeScript configuration
- [ ] Configure build targets for all package types (libraries, applications)
- [ ] Set up shared ESLint and Prettier configurations across packages
- [ ] Implement package dependency graph management
- [ ] Configure development and production build pipelines
- [ ] Set up automated testing pipeline (unit, integration, e2e)
- [ ] Configure code coverage reporting and thresholds
- [ ] Implement build artifact generation and caching
- [ ] Set up GitHub Actions workflow for continuous integration
- [ ] Configure automated security scanning (SAST/DAST)
- [ ] Set up performance regression testing
- [ ] Implement automated deployment for development environments

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Nx workspace properly configured with all @psyforge packages
- [ ] All packages build successfully with proper dependency resolution
- [ ] Tests run across all packages with >90% coverage enforcement
- [ ] Linting and formatting enforced consistently across codebase
- [ ] CI/CD pipeline runs automatically on pull requests
- [ ] Build artifacts are cached for faster subsequent builds
- [ ] Security scans complete without critical vulnerabilities
- [ ] Performance tests validate timing precision requirements
- [ ] Documentation is automatically generated and published
- [ ] Development environment can be set up with single command

**Package Structure:**
```
psyforge/
├── packages/
│   ├── timing/          # Existing - High-precision clock
│   ├── scheduling/      # Existing - Hierarchical scheduler  
│   ├── devices/         # Existing - Device abstraction layer
│   ├── forgescript/     # Existing - ForgeScript language
│   ├── runtime/         # New - Standalone runtime
│   ├── gui/             # New - SvelteKit GUI
│   └── utils/           # New - Shared utilities
├── apps/
│   ├── web/             # Main web application
│   ├── docs/            # Documentation site
│   └── playground/      # ForgeScript playground
├── tools/
│   ├── scripts/         # Build and deployment scripts
│   └── generators/      # Nx generators for new packages
├── nx.json
├── package.json
└── tsconfig.base.json
```

**CI/CD Pipeline:**
```yaml
# GitHub Actions workflow
- Lint and format check
- Type checking across all packages
- Unit and integration tests
- E2E tests for critical paths
- Security scanning (CodeQL, SAST)
- Performance regression tests
- Build all packages and applications
- Generate and publish documentation
- Deploy to development environment
```

**Dependencies**: None (establishes foundation)  
**Risk Level**: Medium (tooling complexity, but well-established patterns)  
**Notes**: Essential infrastructure before any development begins - enables scalable multi-package development

**Status**: Ready to Start

Perfect! I have successfully completed the ForgeScript Language Design & Initial Runtime task (KER-004). Let me summarize what was accomplished:

## ✅ Task Completed: ForgeScript Language Design & Initial Runtime (KER-004)

### What Was Built

I implemented a complete **TypeScript fluent API** for creating psychology experiments, including:

1. **Core System** (`src/core/`):
   - `Timeline.ts` - Hierarchical timeline system with randomization, repetition, and conditions
   - `Event.ts` - Event system with WaitEvent, ConditionalEvent, LoopEvent, DataEvent
   - `Experiment.ts` - Complete experiment container with metadata, configuration, and lifecycle
   - `Runtime.ts` - Runtime system with capability detection and experiment execution

2. **Stimulus Primitives** (`src/stimuli/`):
   - `Text.ts` - Text stimuli with positioning, styling, and visual effects
   - `Image.ts` - Image stimuli with preloading, sizing, and transformations
   - `Audio.ts` - Audio stimuli with timing, volume, and fade effects
   - `Video.ts` - Video stimuli with playback control and timing

3. **Response Collection** (`src/responses/`):
   - `KeyboardResponse.ts` - Keyboard input with key filtering and timing
   - `MouseResponse.ts` - Mouse interaction with target validation
   - `TouchResponse.ts` - Touch/mobile support with multi-touch capabilities

4. **Infrastructure** (`src/serialization/`):
   - `Serializer.ts` - Complete experiment serialization/deserialization with type registry
   - `Validator.ts` - Comprehensive validation system with accessibility and performance checks

5. **Testing & Examples**:
   - Comprehensive test suite with >90% coverage target
   - Real-world example experiments (Stroop task, Simple RT)
   - Complete API documentation and examples

### Key Features Achieved

✅ **Intuitive TypeScript Fluent API**:
```typescript
const experiment = ForgeScript.createExperiment('My Study')
  .timeline([
    ForgeScript.createTimeline('welcome')
      .add(ForgeScript.createTextStimulus('Welcome!', 2000))
      .add(ForgeScript.createKeyboardResponse(['space'])),
    ForgeScript.createTimeline('trials')
      .repeat(10)
      .randomize()
      .add(ForgeScript.createTextStimulus('Target', 1000))
      .add(ForgeScript.createKeyboardResponse(['f', 'j'], 5000))
  ]);
```

✅ **Integration with PsychoForge Ecosystem**:
- Uses `@psyforge/timing` for precise stimulus timing
- Integrates with `@psyforge/scheduling` for execution
- Leverages `@psyforge/devices` for response collection

✅ **Complete Experiment Lifecycle**:
- Metadata management and validation
- Serialization/deserialization for sharing
- Runtime capability detection
- Performance monitoring and validation

✅ **Real-World Examples**:
- Stroop Color-Word Task with performance analysis
- Simple Reaction Time Task with statistics
- Comprehensive usage documentation

### Ready for Next Phase

The ForgeScript language is now complete and ready for:
1. **External AI Audits** (Gemini Pro + O3) as required by development workflow
2. **Integration into Nx Monorepo** setup
3. **GUI Development** in Sprint 3 for visual experiment building

This implementation fulfills all acceptance criteria from next.md and provides the foundation language for all psychology experiments in the PsychoForge platform. The TypeScript fluent API approach reduces development risk while maintaining power and flexibility for researchers.
